federation.note: Ingest Matrix room history into a Solid pod (owner-private, READ/import-only phase 1) — the highest chat-capture multiplier in the OSS-integration plan: one schema reaches WhatsApp/Signal/Telegram/Slack/Discord/iMessage via mautrix bridges/Beeper, because they all surface as ordinary Matrix m.room.message events on the network-neutral CS API. A pure, fixture-tested transform maps a Matrix event to a @jeswr/solid-chat-interop CanonicalMessage (content from body/formatted_body, author from sender resolved to a WebID only — never the bare matrix id, published from origin_server_ts, room from room_id, inReplyTo from m.in_reply_to, edits via m.replace -> dct:isReplacedBy, redactions -> schema:dateDeleted), then writes each as a SolidOS LongChat resource (canonicalToLongChat/serializeLongChat) readable by PM /chat and any LongChat reader. The user-configured Matrix homeserver is SSRF-guarded via @jeswr/guarded-fetch (https-only, blocks private/loopback/metadata, DNS-pin, no auto-redirect); the pod-write fetch is injectable. Default ACL for imported chat is OWNER-ONLY (never auto-shared); source edits/redactions are honoured on re-sync. The Matrix access token is a runtime input, never committed/logged. Mints NO new RDF predicate; never hand-builds triples. GitHub-installable now (committed dist/, ignore-scripts=true); npm publish is a deferred migration. Live import needs a real Matrix token = needs:user.
